summary: tailwindcss-merge typosquats tailwind-merge and executes obfuscated payload on import
details: Package name impersonates the popular `tailwind-merge` library and ships a copied README/API so consumers who mistype the name receive a drop-in lookalike. The published main `dist/bundle-cjs.js` (mirrored in `dist/bundle-mjs.mjs`, `dist/es5/*`, and `src/lib/lib.min.js`) contains a custom string-shuffle obfuscator that reconstructs the `Function` constructor from a shuffled alphabet, decodes a multi-KB payload, and invokes it at module load via `XZs(7942)`. Before invocation it leaks Node privileged primitives to globals (`global['r']=require; global['m']=module;`), giving the decoded payload full ability to spawn processes, read filesystem/credentials, and reach the network. The obfuscated stager is intentionally wired into the source tree: `src/index.ts` ends with `import './lib/lib.min.js';`, a side-effect-only import that ensures the payload runs whenever the library's main entry is required. The legitimate upstream `tailwind-merge` contains no such file. Any project that installs and imports this package (e.g., `import { twMerge } from 'tailwindcss-merge'`) runs attacker-controlled code with the privileges of the Node process.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
